Business Implication: Mean transaction value is stable and well-estimated; budget forecasting is reliable
West significantly more profitable than Central, but effect is small — not the primary driver
Regional profit differences are real; West leads, Central lags — resource allocation should reflect this
Sales do not differ meaningfully across segments — all segments spend similarly per transaction
Segments show no category preference — cross-sell strategy cannot be driven by segment alone
Discounting is unevenly applied across categories — Furniture and Binders receive the heaviest discounts
Discounting is the strongest driver of margin erosion — reducing high discounts is the clearest lever for profitability
